description: Full delegation — you own the goal end-to-end, user just wants results. Triggers: "CEO mode", "get it done", "you decide", "handle everything", "I trust you", "take over", "full authority", "just do it", "搞定 X", "幫我處理", "全權處理", "你決定". Not for: research-only (→ survey), participatory planning (→ dev-flow), or parallel task dispatch.

results.query: ok just get it done, I trust you. implement the monthly settlement scheduler and wire up all the policies. you decide how.
CEO mode. Handle the entire LINE notification integration end to end. Level 3 autonomy, just results. No-go: don't modify the existing webhook system.
handle the go-live gap closure. you have full authority to fix whatever's needed — notifications, scheduler, yesterday close. just don't break existing accounting.
CEO mode, reduce scope. Ship the absolute minimum viable allowance distribution — cut everything non-essential. Scope mode: reduce.
I don't want to think about this anymore. Take over the console migration — figure out what's left, prioritize, execute. Report when done.
investigate whether we should use jemalloc or mimalloc for the accountingd memory issue. research both options and give me a comparison.
I need to add a new API endpoint for client monitoring. Let me plan this out — what size should this be?
debug the memory leak in accountingd — it's using 23GB after batch runs
run these 5 tasks in parallel: update the schema, regenerate types, fix the migration, update the tests, deploy to staging
should we build the notification system as a microservice or keep it in the monolith? I want different perspectives — architect, ops, product.
